mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings and produce negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly equity returns through fundamental earnings channels.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind, triggering margin compression and inventory corrections that force negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration predicts positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent sign flip in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index surpasses 270, elevated inflation expectations trigger aggressive central bank tightening fears, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Markets produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, fundamentally supporting corporate earnings and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains and increase capital goods orders, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST levels show a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 101, it signals that manufacturing capacity utilization is above historical averages, indicating robust economic momentum that supports positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in > 65% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 101, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample validation.
